notes.title: Why the Homepage release contract matters
notes.body: Homepage releases are immutable snapshots. A stable Compose project name, deployment lock, verification step, and current/previous links make releases mutually exclusive, observable, and reversible.

notes.title: Reachability is not freshness
notes.body: A service can answer HTTP successfully while serving stale application data. The runtime contract and Prometheus signals classify endpoint failure separately from old weather reports.

notes.title: Aikido data boundary
notes.body: Homepage receives only aggregate severity counts and freshness. OAuth credentials and detailed findings remain in the server-side adapter boundary.

notes.title: Hardware and service signals
notes.body: Node Exporter supplies durable host metrics, Blackbox Exporter supplies outside-in HTTP availability, and the collector supplies application freshness. Grafana visualizes rather than owns those signals.

questions.prompt: What problem does the Homepage deployment lock primarily prevent?
questions.explanation: The non-blocking lock rejects overlapping deploy and rollback operations, protecting the current/previous release state.

questions.prompt: What happens when a candidate Homepage release fails verification and a previous managed release exists?
choices: All Homepage containers are permanently removed
questions.explanation: Verification failure reactivates the last-known-good managed release without changing Git history.

questions.prompt: Which signal means the collector is reachable but weather reports have stopped arriving?
choices: probe_success equals one and telemetry_source_stale equals one
questions.explanation: Blackbox reachability remains successful while the collector's application-level freshness metric becomes stale.

questions.prompt: Why can a wholly unavailable status on an unsupported workstation return exit code zero?
choices: No confirmed actionable runtime failure was observed on that platform
The schema does not support failures
questions.explanation: Unsupported evidence remains explicit but does not fail automation without a confirmed actionable runtime failure.

questions.prompt: What limits Prometheus storage on brain?
choices: Ninety days or ten gigabytes, whichever is reached first
questions.explanation: Dual time and size retention protects the root logical volume while preserving useful history.

questions.prompt: Why should storage dashboards query the root mount rather than assume the NVMe size?
choices: Node Exporter cannot read NVMe devices
The 1 TB device has only about 98 GB allocated to the root logical volume
questions.explanation: Physical device size and mounted filesystem capacity are different; operational alerts must use the resource that can actually fill.

questions.prompt: Why does Homepage call the local Aikido status adapter instead of the Aikido API directly?
choices: The adapter keeps credentials and finding details out of the browser
questions.explanation: The server-side adapter holds OAuth credentials and publishes only sanitized aggregate counts and freshness.

questions.prompt: What remains the deployment-event recovery source if InfluxDB publication fails?
questions.explanation: The durable local journal is written first; observability publication is best-effort and cannot alter deployment outcomes.

questions.prompt: Why is Node Exporter not published directly to the LAN?
choices: Prometheus scrapes it over the private Compose network
Grafana embeds it in the browser
questions.explanation: Only Prometheus needs the raw exporter endpoint, reducing unnecessary LAN exposure.

questions.prompt: Why is Ecowitt treated as a source plugin rather than the platform boundary?
choices: It makes the weather station upload faster
The normalized envelope and source interface allow future sources without rewriting storage and consumers
questions.explanation: The collector normalizes sources behind a shared envelope so APIs, storage, CLI, and dashboards can extend to ADS-B or other telemetry.
